content.problemStatement:
A policeman wants to catch a thief. Both the policeman and the thief can only move on a line on integer coordinates between 000 and NNN (inclusive).

Initially, the policeman is at a coordinate xxx and the thief is at a coordinate yyy. During each second, each of them must move to the left or right (not necessarily both in the same direction) by distance exactly\textbf{exactly}exactly equal to KKK. No one may go to the left of the coordinate 000 or to the right of NNN. Both the policeman and the thief move simultaneously and they cannot meet while moving, only at the end of each second.

Will the policeman be able to catch the thief if they both move optimally? The thief is caught as soon as the policeman and thief meet at the same position at the same time.
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains four space‑separated integers x, y, K and N.
- 0 ≤ x, y ≤ N
- x ≠ y
- 1 ≤ K ≤ N ≤ 10^9
- Both the policeman (at x) and the thief (at y) move simultaneously each second by exactly K to the left or right, staying inside [0, N].
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "Yes" if the policeman can guarantee to catch the thief, otherwise output "No".

sampleTestCases.explanation:
For each case compute d = |x‑y| and check divisibility by 2K.
1) d=1, 2K=2 → not divisible → No.
2) d=3, 2K=2 → not divisible → No.
3) d=2, 2K=2 → divisible → Yes.
4) d=4, 2K=4 → divisible → Yes.
5) d=6, 2K=6 → divisible → Yes.

1) d=5, 2K=10 → not divisible.
2) d=7, 2K=6 → not divisible.

solution.approach: Both players move exactly K each second. Let d = |x‑y| be the current distance. After a move the distance becomes |d + c·K| where c ∈ {‑2,0,2}. Hence the distance can only change by multiples of 2K, so d mod (2K) never changes. To meet, the distance must become 0, therefore a necessary condition is d % (2K) == 0. This condition is also sufficient: if it holds, both players can always move towards each other (or stay if at a border) decreasing the distance by 2K each second until it reaches 0, and the border constraints never prevent this because a move towards the other side is always legal when the distance is positive. Thus the answer is "Yes" iff |x‑y| is divisible by 2K.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables.
